title: Tunisian Lamb Kebabs
description: A flavorful and aromatic North African-inspired dish featuring tender lamb, rich spices, and a hint of sweetness.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Make sure to trim any excess fat

ingredients.name: Tunisian spice blend (available at most Middle Eastern markets or online)
ingredients.notes: Can substitute with a combination of ground cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and cayenne pepper

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality, extra-virgin olive oil

ingredients.name: Chopped onion
ingredients.notes: Can use red or yellow onion

ingredients.name: Chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Can use fresh cilantro as a substitute

ingredients.name: Honey
ingredients.notes: Can substitute with maple syrup or agave nectar

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together Tunisian spice blend, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the spice mixture.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the lamb kebabs for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, or until cooked to desired level of doneness.
While the lamb is cooking, toast the pita bread or crusty bread slices on the grill or in a toaster.
Once the lamb is cooked, brush with honey and lemon juice.
Serve the lamb kebabs on toasted bread with a sprinkle of chopped parsley and a side of your favorite accompaniments.
Note: If using wooden skewers, make sure to soak them in water for at least 30 minutes before grilling to prevent them from catching fire.
